name: BEADA Cardano stake pool
description: BEADA pool: supporting our planet as a beekeeper, while also educating and training others in the importance of bees for our planet! Additionally, we will donate 20% of pool rewards to charity! Our pool is hosted on hardware at home, powered by solar.

nodes.name: Raspberry Pi 4B 8Gb with heatsinks and fan

metadata.name: BEADA Cardano stake pool
metadata.description: BEADA pool: supporting our planet as a beekeeper, and active member of Climate Neutral Cardano! Additionally, we will donate 25% of pool rewards to charity! Our pool is hosted on hardware at home, powered by solar.

about.me: Hi there, let me introduce myself. In my pool I'm combining 2 large aspects of my life, Cardano and beekeeping. Beekeeping started as a hobby, but turned into a passion. It's very rewarding working with bees, and educating others about bees and their importance and benefit to our environment. I educate people that want to learn more, and help people that want to start beekeeping themselves. My Cardano journey started with Charles Hoskinson's whiteboard video, and that let me down the rabbit hole all the way to starting my own stakepool! It's philisophy and community is what I value most about it.
about.server: Our pool is hosted at home, on energy efficient ARM servers powered by solar on our roof. This ensures truely decentralised, eco-friendly and reliable operation.

metadata.name: ADASRN
metadata.description: Growing wealth together. Keeping fees low with with the lowest pool cost and a 2.5% margin.

about.me: We are Malaysians who want to participate in the Cardano network as we believe DeFi is the future. We are glad to be part of the 100 pools chosen for the second cohort of 2021 to receive the IOG delegation fund. We are grateful to IOG in recognising our contribution to the Cardano ecosystem.
about.server: We have 4 bare metal nodes and 1 Oracle cloud server relay node. The bare metal nodes comprise of 3 relay nodes and 1 block producing node. As fans of the ARM SBCs, 2 of our bare metal relay nodes are run from Raspberry Pi 4B 8GB units whilst our block producer is powered by the Firefly ROC-RK3588S-PC with binaries built from source for the aarch64 architecture. Our final bare metal relay is an AMD64 Linux PC.
